name: azure-cosmos-py
description: Azure Cosmos DB SDK for Python (NoSQL API). Use for document CRUD, queries, containers, and globally distributed data.
capabilities: Create, read, update, and delete documents in Cosmos DB containers
Execute parameterized SQL-like queries with partition key filtering
Provision databases and containers with throughput configuration
Implement async operations for high-throughput scenarios
Handle Cosmos DB exceptions and rate limiting gracefully
Use Azure Identity for credential-based authentication
author: sickn33
securitySummary: This is a prompt-only skill containing documentation and code examples for Azure Cosmos DB SDK. Static analysis found 0 security issues with a risk score of 0/100. The skill provides legitimate database operation patterns using official Azure SDK libraries. Network and environment access patterns shown are standard Azure authentication flows requiring user-provided credentials.
valueStatement: Working with globally distributed databases requires understanding partition keys and efficient queries. This skill provides production-ready patterns for Azure Cosmos DB operations using the official Python SDK.
userTitle: Build Cosmos DB applications with Python SDK
limitations: Only supports NoSQL API, not MongoDB, Cassandra, or Gremlin APIs
Requires valid Azure Cosmos DB account endpoint and credentials
Partition key design must be planned before container creation
Cross-partition queries incur higher latency and request units

useCases.targetUser: Backend developers building multi-region e-commerce platforms
useCases.title: E-commerce Product Catalog
useCases.description: Build a globally distributed product catalog with partition key optimization for fast lookups by category and efficient range queries for price filtering.

useCases.targetUser: IoT solution architects handling high-volume device telemetry
useCases.title: IoT Telemetry Storage
useCases.description: Store and query time-series sensor data using hierarchical partition keys for tenant and device isolation with automatic global replication.

useCases.targetUser: Full-stack developers building scalable web applications
useCases.title: User Session Management
useCases.description: Implement low-latency session storage with automatic failover across Azure regions using Cosmos DB consistency levels and TTL policies.

promptTemplates.title: Basic CRUD Operations
promptTemplates.prompt: Create a Python script that connects to Azure Cosmos DB and performs create, read, update, and delete operations on a container storing user profiles with partition key /user_id.
promptTemplates.scenario: Beginner learning core Cosmos DB operations

promptTemplates.title: Parameterized Query with Filtering
promptTemplates.prompt: Write a query function that retrieves products under a maximum price within a specific category partition using parameterized SQL to prevent injection and improve caching.

promptTemplates.title: Async High-Throughput Batch Insert
promptTemplates.prompt: Implement an async Python function that batches and inserts 1000 items into Cosmos DB using the async client with proper error handling and retry logic for rate limiting.
promptTemplates.scenario: Advanced developer building high-volume data pipelines

promptTemplates.title: Container Provisioning with Partition Strategy
promptTemplates.prompt: Generate a setup script that creates a Cosmos DB container with hierarchical partition keys, provisioned throughput of 5000 RU/s, and appropriate indexing policies for a multi-tenant event logging system.

bestPractices: Always specify partition key for point reads and queries to minimize latency and cost
Use parameterized queries instead of string concatenation to prevent injection attacks
Design partition keys for even data distribution to avoid hot partitions
antiPatterns: Performing cross-partition queries without enable_cross_partition_query flag
Using read_all_items without partition key filtering in production workloads
Creating containers without planning partition key strategy upfront

faq.question: What is the minimum throughput for a Cosmos DB container?
faq.answer: The minimum provisioned throughput is 400 RU/s for a single partition. Serverless mode is also available for unpredictable workloads.

faq.question: How do I handle 429 rate limiting errors?
faq.answer: Catch CosmosHttpResponseError with status code 429 and retry after the duration specified in the x-ms-retry-after-ms header.

faq.question: Can I change the partition key after container creation?
faq.answer: No, partition keys are immutable. You must migrate data to a new container if you need to change the partition strategy.

faq.question: What authentication methods does the SDK support?
faq.answer: The SDK supports connection string authentication and Azure Identity-based authentication using DefaultAzureCredential for managed identities.

faq.question: How do I enable async operations in the SDK?
faq.answer: Import CosmosClient from azure.cosmos.aio and use async context managers with await for all database operations.

faq.question: What consistency levels are available in Cosmos DB?
faq.answer: Cosmos DB offers five levels: strong, bounded staleness, session, consistent prefix, and eventual, trading off latency and consistency.
